1.notes: Westward Ho - Medium par 4 opening hole. Wide fairway with fescue rough. Bunkers guard approach.

2.notes: Plateau - Long par 3 to elevated green. Wind exposed. Deep bunkers protect green.

3.notes: Peconic - Long par 4 with slight dogleg. Strategic bunkering requires placement off tee.

4.notes: Pump House - Strong par 4. Demands accurate drive to set up approach to well-bunkered green.

5.notes: Montauk - Long par 5 with significant dogleg. Reachable in two for long hitters taking on the bunkers.

6.notes: Pond - Long par 4 with water hazard. Approach must carry to well-protected green.

7.notes: Redan - Famous par 3 modeled after North Berwick's Redan. Green slopes sharply front-right to back-left. Deep bunkers protect.

8.notes: Lowlands - Medium par 4 in the valley. Wide fairway but approach demands precision.

9.notes: Ben Nevis - Demanding uphill par 4 to green by clubhouse. One of the toughest finishes to a front nine.

10.notes: Eastward Ho - Par 4 heading back from clubhouse. Strategic options for angle of approach.

11.notes: Hill Head - Short but iconic par 3. Elevated, severely bunkered green. Wind plays havoc with club selection.

12.notes: Tuckahoe - Long par 4 with dogleg. Requires strategic positioning to approach the green.

13.notes: Road Side - Medium par 4 with subtle bends. Green complex demands precision.

14.notes: Thom's Elbow - Monster par 4. Contoured with strategic bunkers. Demands two perfect shots.

15.notes: Sebonac - Medium par 4 playing uphill. Wind often a factor. Green well protected.

16.notes: Shinnecock - Long par 5 with risk-reward second shot. Bunkers guard layup and green.

17.notes: Eden - Par 3 inspired by St Andrews Eden hole. Green slopes and bunkers demand precision.

18.notes: Home - Classic uphill finishing hole to clubhouse. Iconic approach with bunkers guarding green. Scene of Corey Pavin's famous 4-wood in 1995.

1.notes: Medium par 4 opener. Tillinghast design with strategic bunkering. Sets tone for challenging round.

2.notes: Long par 4 with gentle dogleg. Requires placement off tee to set up approach angle.

3.notes: Long par 3. Tillinghast's challenging one-shotter with severe bunkering.

4.notes: Strong par 4. Requires accuracy off tee to avoid bunkers and set up approach.

5.notes: Reachable par 5 for long hitters. Risk-reward second shot over bunkers.

6.notes: Short par 4 but demanding. Precision required to hit small target.

7.notes: Short par 3 but one of golf's most feared. Deep bunkers surround tiny, sloping green.

8.notes: Long par 4. Demands two strong shots to reach well-protected green.

9.notes: Monster par 5 (played as par 4 in majors at 565 yds). One of golf's most difficult holes.

10.notes: Par 3 to start back nine. Demands accuracy with bunkers surrounding green.

11.notes: Medium par 4 with subtle contours. Approach must find correct portion of green.

12.notes: Massive par 5 at 640 yards - 6th longest hole in major championship history. Demands three quality shots.

13.notes: Par 3 with severe green contours. Club selection critical.

14.notes: Long par 4 with dogleg. Requires positioning off tee to set up demanding approach.

15.notes: Strong par 4. Green contours demand precise approach.

16.notes: Long par 4 (par 5 for members). Demands two powerful shots.

17.notes: Long par 4 with subtle dogleg. Positioning critical for approach to difficult green.

18.notes: Classic finisher. Scene of Mickelson's infamous double bogey in 2006. Demands precision under pressure.

1.notes: Opening par 4 with dogleg. Creek runs along left side. Elevated tee provides vista of course.

2.notes: Long par 4 with gentle dogleg. Perry Maxwell rolls throughout.

3.notes: Strong par 4 with Maxwell's characteristic undulating green. Trees frame both sides.

4.notes: Shorter par 4 with creek. Risk-reward off tee - cut corner over water for shorter approach.

5.notes: Monstrous par 5 at 656 yards for 2022 PGA. Creek crosses in front of green.

6.notes: Par 3 over water. Demanding green complex with severe slopes.

7.notes: Long par 4 with dogleg. Creek runs along left side. Maxwell rolls on approach.

8.notes: Long par 3 uphill. One of the most demanding par 3s in championship golf.

9.notes: Par 4 finishing front nine. Creek crosses fairway. Uphill to clubhouse.

10.notes: Back nine opener. Downhill with creek along left. Tight landing area.

11.notes: Short par 3 but deceptive. Creek runs behind green. Wind affects club selection.

12.notes: Long par 4 bending left. Classic Maxwell design with undulating fairway.

13.notes: Long par 5 at 632 yards. Shared fairway with 16th creates unique visual. Creek throughout.

14.notes: Long par 3 over water. One of most photographed holes in Oklahoma.

15.notes: Medium par 4 with subtle contours. Maxwell rolls demand precise approach.

16.notes: Long par 4 (par 5 for members). Shared fairway with 13th. Creek along left.

17.notes: Medium par 4 setting up dramatic finish. Creek crosses in front of green.

18.notes: Classic uphill finishing hole. Creek along left, bunkers right. Dramatic approach to green by clubhouse.
